text.title: Impl Plan Task Format Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Impl Plan Task Format. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every TASK file carries YAML front-matter with id, est_tokens, status, depends_on, acceptance, owner.
rule.rationale: Missing front-matter means agents cannot route the task.

rule.statement: Body has exactly five sections in order: Goal, Inputs, Steps, Acceptance, Notes.
rule.rationale: Fixed sections let agents parse without LLM intervention.

rule.statement: Filename is TASK_NNN-kebab-case-title.md; capitals and spaces rejected.
rule.rationale: Slugs are URLs and file paths; capitals break case-sensitive filesystems.

rule.statement: Each TASK names exactly one primary component (file path, module, or service).
Acceptance section lists ≥1 binary check; 'works correctly' rejected.
est_tokens front-matter field is required and ≤ 100k (see impl-plan-100k-rule).
rule.rationale: Tasks without estimates cannot be scheduled against a budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-impl-plan-task-format.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
